//: TEMPLATE — skill.json shape. Prescribed by skills playbook + AGENTS.md Rule 2 (cross-skill schema parity). Fields below are required unless marked optional. Validator: each skill's evals/run-static-checks.sh enforces this shape — every field a skill ships must be checked there, not just the fields the writer remembered.
name: <skill-name-must-match-directory>
description: <one-line description used by catalogs and routing>
//status: Installable skills in agent-skill-kit use published status; prerelease maturity is communicated by the repository release tag.
//maintainers: Each entry MUST match ^@[A-Za-z0-9-]+$ or ^@[A-Za-z0-9-]+/[A-Za-z0-9-]+$ (Rule 4). Opaque strings like 'justin' fail the static check because they cannot be resolved to a reviewer.

inspired_by.name: <source title>
inspired_by.author: <author or organization>
inspired_by.kind: <kind — e.g. book, paper, talk, article, blog, repo>
inspired_by.contribution: <one-line: what this source contributed to the skill>

//inspired_by: Optional; encouraged when the skill draws on cited sources — the schema accepts an absent or empty list. The 'kind' is free-form — e.g. book, paper, talk, article, blog, repo. 'contribution' must be a single line; 'playbooks' references either a routed value (intent from references/intent-router.csv, surface from a per-intent CSV under references/intents/, or surface from references/surface-router.csv) or an 'all'/'<intent>-intent' marker depending on skill structure.
//SKILL.md-pairing: This file pairs with SKILL.md (frontmatter: name, description, license). The name here MUST match SKILL.md's frontmatter name AND the directory name. trigger-evals.json's 'skill' field MUST also match.
